preamble: Monophthongization of the diphthong [uo] to the open front unrounded vowel [a] through vowel merger with lowering and fronting. This shift occurs in various Romance and Sino-Tibetan languages, representing a common pathway in complex diphthong reduction.

examples.note: In Wu Chinese, the back-rounded to front-open diphthong [uo] monophthongizes to [a] in certain phonological contexts.
In some Romance dialects, complex diphthongs like [uo] reduce to low vowel [a] in certain phonological environments.
